evals.prompt: I want to build a REST API on Cloudflare Workers using Python and FastAPI. It needs a D1 database for storing bookmarks (url, title, created_at). Set up the project structure, wrangler config, and a basic CRUD handler.
evals.expected_output: Complete project setup with wrangler.jsonc (python_workers flag, python_dedicated_snapshot, D1 binding), pyproject.toml, FastAPI ASGI bridge using request.js_object, FFI boundary layer with HAS_PYODIDE guard, D1 row conversion with .to_py()
Identifies that results.results is JsProxy, shows the fix with .to_py() conversion, recommends boundary layer pattern

evals.prompt: I have a Python Worker with D1 and Queues. How do I set up pytest to test it? The Worker code imports `from pyodide.ffi import to_js` and `import js` so it crashes when I try to import it in regular Python.
evals.expected_output: HAS_PYODIDE guard pattern, MockD1/MockQueue implementations, pyproject.toml pytest config, Pyodide fakes for testing FFI branches, three-tier testing strategy

evals.prompt: I'm porting an existing Python service to Cloudflare Workers. Can I keep using synchronous HTTP clients like `requests`, `urllib3`, or `httpx.Client`, or do Python Workers require async-only HTTP? Show the recommended guidance and tradeoffs.
evals.expected_output: Explains that a subset of sync HTTP clients is demonstrated to work in Python Workers (`requests`, `urllib3.PoolManager`, `httpx.Client`), avoids claiming async-only HTTP, recommends `workers.fetch()` or async clients such as `httpx.AsyncClient` for concurrent fan-out/latency-sensitive code, notes sync clients block the handler and that raw stdlib/socket HTTP paths are not generally demonstrated
